title: The story of the one line fix
summary: Picture yourself, an engineer working at the hottest distributed microservices de jour, assigned to fix a bug. You jump into an unfamiliar codebase and quickly locate the line where the problem occurred. summary: The Go compiler’s SSA backend contains a facility to produce HTML debugging output of the compilation phases. This post covers how to print the SSA output for function and methods. title: Ensmallening Go binaries by prohibiting comparisons
summary: Conventional wisdom dictates that the larger the number of types declared in a Go program, the larger the resulting binary. title: Mid-stack inlining in Go
summary: In the previous post I discussed how leaf inlining allows the Go compiler to reduce the overhead of function calls and extend optimisation opportunities across function boundaries. In this post I’ll discuss the limits of inlining and leaf vs mid-stack inlining. The limits of inlining Inlining a function into its caller removes the call’s overhead […]
